### domain entity does not exist `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002`

#### Added
- Individual: [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The term was added to the ontology on the assumption it was a valid domain entity, but it turns out the entity does not exist in reality."@en 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "This obsolesence reason should be used conservatively. For example: Obsoleting class that describes a breed of cow based on a record in an existing database, that was later retracted as faulty (breed does not exist). Do not use this term to obsolete a historic concept (that was once valid, but not anymore). "@en 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-4142-7153](http://orcid.org/0000-0002-4142-7153) 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/information-artifact-ontology/ontology-metadata/issues/136" 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "domain entity does not exist" 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) Type [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225)

### entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001`

#### Added
- Class: [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Julius Caesar"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Verdi&rsquo;s Requiem"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the Second World War"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "your body mass index"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: In all areas of empirical inquiry we encounter general terms of two sorts. First are general terms which refer to universals or types:animaltuberculosissurgical procedurediseaseSecond, are general terms used to refer to groups of entities which instantiate a given universal but do not correspond to the extension of any subuniversal of that universal because there is nothing intrinsic to the entities in question by virtue of which they &ndash; and only they &ndash; are counted as belonging to the given group. Examples are: animal purchased by the Emperortuberculosis diagnosed on a Wednesdaysurgical procedure performed on a patient from Stockholmperson identified as candidate for clinical trial #2056-555person who is signatory of Form 656-PPVpainting by Leonardo da VinciSuch terms, which represent what are called &lsquo;specializations&rsquo; in [81"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "entity"@en

### function `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034`

#### Added
- Class: [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of a hammer to drive in nails"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of a heart pacemaker to regulate the beating of a heart through electricity"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of amylase in saliva to break down starch into sugar"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: In the past, we have distinguished two varieties of function, artifactual function and biological function. These are not asserted subtypes of BFO:function however, since the same function &ndash; for example: to pump, to transport &ndash; can exist both in artifacts and in biological entities. The asserted subtypes of function that would be needed in order to yield a separate monoheirarchy are not artifactual function, biological function, etc., but rather transporting function, pumping function, etc."@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "function"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) SubClassOf [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) 


### function of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this catalysis function is a function of this enzyme"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between a function and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A function inheres in its bearer at all times for which the function exists, however the function need not be realized at all the times that the function exists."@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "function_of"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "is function of"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "function of"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) InverseOf [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) Domain [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) SubPropertyOf: [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052)

### generically dependent continuant `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031`

#### Added
- Class: [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "The entries in your database are patterns instantiated as quality instances in your hard drive. The database itself is an aggregate of such patterns. When you create the database you create a particular instance of the generically dependent continuant type database. Each entry in the database is an instance of the generically dependent continuant type IAO: information content entity."@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the pdf file on your laptop, the pdf file that is a copy thereof on my laptop"@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the sequence of this protein molecule; the sequence that is a copy thereof in that protein molecule."@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "generically dependent continuant"@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) SubClassOf [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002)

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this apple is bearer of this red color"@en 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this vase is bearer of this fragility"@en

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A bearer can have many dependents, and its dependents can exist for different periods of time, but none of its dependents can exist when the bearer does not exist."@en

### has component `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) 

- [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "w 'has component' p if w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type." 

- [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has component"@en 

- [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) SubPropertyOf: [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) 


### has component activity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002017`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002017) 

- [has component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002017) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has component activity" 

- [has component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002017) SubPropertyOf: [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) 


### has component process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) 

- [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "w 'has process component' p if p and w are processes,  w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type." 

- [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has component process" 

- [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) Domain [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) Range [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) SubPropertyOf: [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180)

#### Removed
- AnnotationProperty: [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref)

### has disposition `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091) 

- [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a disposition, in which the disposition specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has disposition"@en 

- [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091) InverseOf [disposition of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000092) 

- [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091) Domain [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) 

- [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091) Range [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) 

- [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091) SubPropertyOf: [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) 


### has effector activity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025) 

- [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has effector activity" 

-  Functional: [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025) 

- [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025) SubPropertyOf: [has component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002017) 


### has exact synonym `http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym`
#### Removed
- AnnotationProperty: [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym)

### has function `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this enzyme has function this catalysis function (more colloquially: this enzyme has this catalysis function)"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a function, in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A bearer can have many functions, and its functions can exist for different periods of time, but none of its functions can exist when the bearer does not exist. A function need not be realized at all the times that the function exists."@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "has_function"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has function"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) Domain [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) Range [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) SubPropertyOf: [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) 


### has input `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) 

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p has input c iff: p is a process, c is a material entity, c is a participant in p, c is present at the start of p, and the state of c is modified during p." 

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [Christopher J. Mungall](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has input"@en 

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) InverseOf [input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002352) 

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) Domain [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) SubPropertyOf: [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057) 


### has member `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002351`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has member](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002351) 

- [has member](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002351) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "has member is a mereological relation between a collection and an item." 

- [has member](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002351) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has member"@en 

-  Irreflexive: [has member](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002351)

### has negative regulatory component activity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has negative regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014) 

- [has negative regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relationship that holds between a GO molecular function and a component of that molecular function that negatively regulates the activity of the whole.  More formally, A 'has regulatory component activity' B iff :A and B are GO molecular functions (GO_0003674), A has_component B and A is negatively regulated by B." 

- [has negative regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has negative regulatory component activity" 

- [has negative regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014) SubPropertyOf: [has regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002013) 

- [has negative regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014) SubPropertyOf: [negatively regulated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002335) 


### has part `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) 

- [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a core relation that holds between a whole and its part"@en 

- [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has part"@en 

- [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) SubPropertyOf: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

-  Transitive: [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) 


### has part structure that is capable of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has part structure that is capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584) 

- [has part structure that is capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "s 'has part structure that is capable of' p if and only if there exists some part x such that s 'has part' x and x 'capable of' p" 

- [has part structure that is capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [Christopher J. Mungall](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [has part structure that is capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has part structure that is capable of" 

- [has part structure that is capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584) SubPropertyOf: [functionally related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002328) 

- [has part structure that is capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584) SubPropertyOf: [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) 

- [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) o [capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215) SubPropertyOf: [has part structure that is capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002584) 


### has participant `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057) 

- [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between a process and a continuant, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process"@en 

- [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has participant"@en

- [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057) Range [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) o [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057) SubPropertyOf: [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057) 


### has positive regulatory component activity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has positive regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015) 

- [has positive regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relationship that holds between a GO molecular function and a component of that molecular function that positively regulates the activity of the whole.  More formally, A 'has regulatory component activity' B iff :A and B are GO molecular functions (GO_0003674), A has_component B and A is positively regulated by B." 

- [has positive regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has positive regulatory component activity" 

- [has positive regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015) SubPropertyOf: [has regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002013) 

- [has positive regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015) SubPropertyOf: [positively regulated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002336) 


### has quality `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) 

- [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this apple has quality this red color"@en 

- [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a quality, in which the quality specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A bearer can have many qualities, and its qualities can exist for different periods of time, but none of its qualities can exist when the bearer does not exist."@en 

- [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "has_quality"@en 

- [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has quality"@en 

- [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) Range [quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000019) 

- [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) SubPropertyOf: [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053)

### hierarchical clustering `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042`

#### Added
- Class: [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A hierarchical clustering is a data transformation which achieves a class discovery objective, which takes as input data item and builds a hierarchy of clusters. The traditional representation of this hierarchy is a tree (visualized by a dendrogram), with the individual input objects at one end (leaves) and a single cluster containing every object at the other (root)."@en 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "hierarchical clustering"@en 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) SubClassOf [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) SubClassOf ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178))

#### Added
- Class: [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a chair"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a heart"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a leg"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a molecule"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a spatial region"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an atom"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an orchestra."@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an organism"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the bottom right portion of a human torso"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the interior of your mouth"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "b is an independent continuant = Def. b is a continuant which is such that there is no c and no t such that b s-depends_on c at t. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [017-002])"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "independent continuant"@en 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) DisjointWith [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) DisjointWith [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) SubClassOf [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) only [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004)

### information content entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030`

#### Added
- Class: [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [editor preferred label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "information content entity"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Examples of information content entites include journal articles, data, graphical layouts, and graphs."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [ready for release](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122) 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A generically dependent continuant that is about some thing."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2014-03-10: The use of &quot;thing&quot; is intended to be general enough to include universals and configurations (see https://groups.google.com/d/msg/information-ontology/GBxvYZCk1oc/-L6B5fSBBTQJ)."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "information_content_entity 'is_encoded_in' some digital_entity in obi before split (040907). information_content_entity 'is_encoded_in' some physical_document in obi before split (040907).

Previous. An information content entity is a non-realizable information entity that 'is encoded in' some digital or physical entity."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Chris Stoeckert"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "OBI_0000142"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "information content entity"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) SubClassOf [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001)

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "This document is about information artifacts and their representations"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A (currently) primitive relation that relates an information artifact to an entity."@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "7/6/2009 Alan Ruttenberg. Following discussion with Jonathan Rees, and introduction of &quot;mentions&quot; relation. Weaken the is_about relationship to be primitive. 

We will try to build it back up by elaborating the various subproperties that are more precisely defined.

Some currently missing phenomena that should be considered &quot;about&quot; are predications - &quot;The only person who knows the answer is sitting beside me&quot; , Allegory, Satire, and other literary forms that can be topical without explicitly mentioning the topic."@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "person:Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "Smith, Ceusters, Ruttenberg, 2000 years of philosophy"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "is about"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) Domain [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030)

### is specified input of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000295`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [is specified input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000295) 

- [is specified input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000295) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relation between a completely executed planned process and a continuant participating in that process that is not created during the process. The presence of the continuant during the process is explicitly specified in the plan specification which the process realizes the concretization of." 

- [is specified input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000295) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Alan Ruttenberg"@en

- [is specified input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000295) Range [completely executed planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000035) 

- [is specified input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000295) SubPropertyOf: [participates in](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000056)

### k-means clustering `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041`

#### Added
- Class: [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A k-means clustering is a data transformation which achieves a class discovery or partitioning objective, which takes as input a collection of objects (represented as points in multidimensional space) and which partitions them into a specified number k of clusters. The algorithm attempts to find the centers of natural clusters in the data. The most common form of the algorithm starts by partitioning the input points into k initial sets, either at random or using some heuristic data. It then calculates the mean point, or centroid, of each set. It constructs a new partition by associating each point with the closest centroid. Then the centroids are recalculated for the new clusters, and the algorithm repeated by alternate applications of these two steps until convergence, which is obtained when the points no longer switch clusters (or alternatively centroids are no longer changed)."@en 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Elisabetta Manduchi" 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Philippe Rocca-Serra" 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "k-means clustering"@en 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) SubClassOf [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) SubClassOf [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) SubClassOf ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178)) 


### k-nearest neighbors `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727`

#### Added
- Class: [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A k-nearest neighbors is a data transformation which achieves a class discovery or partitioning objective, in which an input data object with vector y is assigned to a class label based upon the k closest training data set points to y; where k is the largest value that class label is assigned."@en 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "k-nearest neighbors"@en 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) SubClassOf [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) SubClassOf [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) SubClassOf ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178))

### leave one out cross validation method `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033`

#### Added
- Class: [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "is a data transformation :  leave-one-out cross-validation (LOOCV) involves using a single observation from the original sample as the validation data, and the remaining observations as the training data. This is repeated such that each observation in the sample is used once as the validation data"@en 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Person:Helen Parkinson" 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "leave one out cross validation method"@en 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) SubClassOf [statistical model validation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000792)

### material entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040`

#### Added
- Class: [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a flame"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a forest fire"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a human being"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a hurricane"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a photon"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a puff of smoke"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a sea wave"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a tornado"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an aggregate of human beings."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an energy wave"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an epidemic"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the undetached arm of a human being"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An independent continuant that is spatially extended whose identity is independent of that of other entities and can be maintained through time."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: Material entities (continuants) can preserve their identity even while gaining and losing material parts. Continuants are contrasted with occurrents, which unfold themselves in successive temporal parts or phases [60"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: Object, Fiat Object Part and Object Aggregate are not intended to be exhaustive of Material Entity. Users are invited to propose new subcategories of Material Entity."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: &lsquo;Matter&rsquo; is intended to encompass both mass and energy (we will address the ontological treatment of portions of energy in a later version of BFO). A portion of matter is anything that includes elementary particles among its proper or improper parts: quarks and leptons, including electrons, as the smallest particles thus far discovered; baryons (including protons and neutrons) at a higher level of granularity; atoms and molecules at still higher levels, forming the cells, organs, organisms and other material entities studied by biologists, the portions of rock studied by geologists, the fossils studied by paleontologists, and so on.Material entities are three-dimensional entities (entities extended in three spatial dimensions), as contrasted with the processes in which they participate, which are four-dimensional entities (entities extended also along the dimension of time).According to the FMA, material entities may have immaterial entities as parts &ndash; including the entities identified below as sites; for example the interior (or &lsquo;lumen&rsquo;) of your small intestine is a part of your body. BFO 2.0 embodies a decision to follow the FMA here."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "material entity"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) DisjointWith [immaterial entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000141) 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) SubClassOf [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) 


### material supplier `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002989`

#### Added
- Class: [material supplier](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002989)

- [material supplier](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002989) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Rebecca Jackson"

- [material supplier](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002989) EquivalentTo ([Homo sapiens](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_9606) or [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245)) and ([has role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000087) some [material supplier role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000018))

- [metabolic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008152) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A cellular process consisting of the biochemical pathways by which a living organism transforms chemical substances. This includes including anabolism (biosynthetic process) and catabolism (catabolic process). Metabolic processes includes the transformation of small molecules, as well  macromolecular processes such as DNA repair and replication, protein synthesis and degradation." 
  - [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "ISBN:0198547684" 

  - [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

- [metabolic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008152) [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Reactome:R-HSA-1430728" 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Metabolism" 

- [metabolic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008152) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Note that metabolic processes do not include single functions or processes such as protein-protein interactions, protein-nucleic acids, nor receptor-ligand interactions."

- [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Class has all its metadata, but is either not guaranteed to be in its final location in the asserted IS_A hierarchy or refers to another class that is not complete."@en 

- [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "metadata complete"@en 

- [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120) Type [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) 

-  DifferentIndividuals: [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120), [organizational term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000121), [ready for release](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122), [metadata incomplete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000123), [uncurated](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000124), [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125), [to be replaced with external ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000423), [requires discussion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000428)

#### Added

Function in this sense denotes an action, or activity, that a gene product (or a complex) performs."

- [molecularly interacts with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002436) SubPropertyOf: [interacts with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002434)

### mutually_spatially_disjoint_with `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171`
#### Removed
- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [expand assertion to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000425) "Class: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Nothing&gt; EquivalentTo: (BFO_0000050 some ?X) and (BFO_0000050 some ?Y)" 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "RO:0002171" 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [has_obo_namespace](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasOBONamespace) "uberon" 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "mutually_spatially_disjoint_with" 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [is_class_level](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_class_level) true 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [is_metadata_tag](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_metadata_tag) true 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [shorthand](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#shorthand) "mutually_spatially_disjoint_with" 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "mutually_spatially_disjoint_with" 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [see also](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#seeAlso) "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/wiki/Part-disjointness-Design-Pattern"^^[anyURI](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI) 

- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [see also](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#seeAlso) [spatially disjoint from](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002163) 

#### Added
- [mutually_spatially_disjoint_with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [defined by construct](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0002000) "PREFIX owl: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#&gt;
PREFIX part_of: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050&gt;
PREFIX mutually_spatially_disjoint_with: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171&gt;
CONSTRUCT {
  part_of: a owl:ObjectProperty .
  [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty part_of: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom ?x ;
    owl:disjointWith [
      a owl:Restriction ;
      owl:onProperty part_of: ;
      owl:someValuesFrom ?y
    ]
  ]
}
WHERE {
  ?x mutually_spatially_disjoint_with: ?y .
}" 


### named class expression `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421`

#### Added
- Individual: [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [editor preferred label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "named class expression"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A named class expression is a logical expression that is given a name. The name can be used in place of the expression."@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "named class expressions are used in order to have more concise logical definition but their extensions may not be interesting classes on their own. In languages such as OWL, with no provisions for macros, these show up as actuall classes. Tools may with to not show them as such, and to replace uses of the macros with their expansions"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "named class expression"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) Type [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409)

- [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212) SubPropertyOf: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211)

### negatively regulates characteristic `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002`

- [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relationship that holds between a process and a characteristic in which process (P) negatively regulates characteristic (C) iff:&nbsp;P results in a decrease in the intensity or magnitude of C." 

- [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "negatively regulates characteristic" 

- [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) SubPropertyOf: [regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019000) 

- [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212) o [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) SubPropertyOf: [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) o [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) SubPropertyOf: [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) 


### never in taxon `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161`
#### Removed
- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "tooth SubClassOf 'never in taxon' value 'Aves'" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [expand assertion to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000425) "Class: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Nothing&gt; EquivalentTo: ?X and (RO_0002162 some ?Y)" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [expand assertion to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000425) "Class: ?X DisjointWith: RO_0002162 some ?Y " 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "RO:0002161" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [has_obo_namespace](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasOBONamespace) "uberon" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "never_in_taxon" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [is_class_level](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_class_level) true 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [is_metadata_tag](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_metadata_tag) true 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [shorthand](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#shorthand) "never_in_taxon" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "S never_in_taxon T iff: S SubClassOf in_taxon only not T." 
  - [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:20973947" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "never_in_taxon" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [see also](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#seeAlso) [Taxon-constraints](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/wiki/Taxon-constraints) 

#### Added
- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [Christopher J. Mungall](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [defined by construct](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0002000) "PREFIX rdfs: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#&gt;
PREFIX owl: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#&gt;
PREFIX in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162&gt;
PREFIX never_in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161&gt;
CONSTRUCT {
  in_taxon: a owl:ObjectProperty .
  ?x owl:disjointWith [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty in_taxon: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom ?taxon
  ] .
  ?x rdfs:subClassOf [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty in_taxon: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom [
      a owl:Class ;
      owl:complementOf ?taxon
    ]
  ] .
}
WHERE {
  ?x never_in_taxon: ?taxon .
}"

When part of a plan specification the concretization is realized in a planned process in which the bearer tries to effect the world so that the process endpoint is achieved."@en

### obsolescence reason specification `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225`

#### Added
- Class: [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [editor preferred label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "obsolescence reason specification"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The reason for which a term has been deprecated. The allowed values come from an enumerated list of predefined terms. See the specification of these instances for more detailed definitions of each enumerated value."@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "The creation of this class has been inspired in part by Werner Ceusters' paper, Applying evolutionary terminology auditing to the Gene Ontology."@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Melanie Courtot"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "obsolescence reason specification"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) EquivalentTo {[failed exploratory term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000103) , [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) , [terms merged](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000227) , [term imported](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000228) , [term split](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000229) , [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000)} 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) SubClassOf [data about an ontology part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000102)

### organism `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000022`

#### Added
- Class: [organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000022) 

- [organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000022) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A material entity that is a maximal functionally integrated unit that develops from a program encoded in a genome"@en 

- [organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000022) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "organism"@en

### organization `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245`

#### Added
- Class: [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An entity that can bear roles, has members, and has a set of organization rules. Members of organizations are either organizations themselves or individual people. Members can bear specific organization member roles that are determined in the organization rules. The organization rules also determine how decisions are made on behalf of the organization by the organization members."@en 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Alan Ruttenberg" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Bjoern Peters" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Philippe Rocca-Serra" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Susanna Sansone" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "organization"@en 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) SubClassOf [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040)

### out of scope `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000`

#### Added
- Individual: [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The term was added to the ontology on the assumption it was in scope, but it turned out later that it was not."@en 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "This obsolesence reason should be used conservatively. Typical valid examples in disease ontologies are: unnecessary class grouping; mistaked creation of a disease associated to a phenotype."@en 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0001-5208-3432](http://orcid.org/0000-0001-5208-3432) 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/information-artifact-ontology/ontology-metadata/issues/77" 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "out of scope" 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) Type [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) 


### overlaps `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131`

#### Added

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "overlaps"@en 

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) SubPropertyOf: [mereotopologically related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002323) 

- [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) o [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) SubPropertyOf: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

- [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) o [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) SubPropertyOf: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) o [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) SubPropertyOf: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

-  Symmetric: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131)

### pN1c (colon) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002275`

- [pN1c (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002275) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic lymph node stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition indicating tumor deposit(s), i.e., satellites in the subserosa, or in non-peritonealized pericolic or perirectal soft tissue without regional lymph node metastasis."@en 

- [pN1c (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002275) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pN1c (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002275) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pN1c (colon)" 

- [pN1c (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002275) Type [pathologic lymph node stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002270)

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is 3 cm or less in greatest dimension, surrounded by lung or visceral pleura without bronchoscopic evidence of invasion more proximal than the lobar bronchus (i.e., not in the main bronchus)."@en 

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT1 (lung)" 

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232)

- [pT1a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002259) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is limited to one ovary; capsule intact, no tumor on ovarian surface and no malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en 

- [pT1a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002259) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT1a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002259) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT1a (ovary)" 

- [pT1a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002259) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256)

- [pT1b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002260) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is limited to both ovaries; capsule intact, no tumor on ovarian surface and no malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en 

- [pT1b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002260) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

### pT1c (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002261`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT1c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002261) 

- [pT1c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002261) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is limited to one or both ovaries with capsule ruptured, tumor on ovarian surface, or malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en 

- [pT1c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002261) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

- [pT1c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002261) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256)

### pT2 (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002248`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002248) 

- [pT2 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002248) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 7 cm in greatest dimension and limited to the kidney."@en 

- [pT2 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002248) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

### pT2 (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 3 cm but not more than 7 cm or the tumor has any of the following features: involves main bronchus, 2 cm or more distal to the carina, invades visceral pleura, associated with atelectasis or obstructive pneumonitis that extends to the hilar region but does not involve the entire lung."@en 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT2 (lung)" 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232) 


### pT2 (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262`

- Individual: [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor involves one or both ovaries with pelvic extension."@en 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT2 (ovary)" 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256)

- Individual: [pT2a (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002239) 

- [pT2a (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002239) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 3 cm but not more than 5 cm in greatest dimension."@en

- [pT2a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002263) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has extension and/or implants on uterus and/or tube(s) and no malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2b (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002240) 

- [pT2b (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002240) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 5 cm but not more than 7 cm in greatest dimension."@en

- [pT2b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002264) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has extension to other pelvic tissues and no malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en

- Individual: [pT2c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002265) 

- [pT2c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002265) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has pelvic extension with malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en

### pT3 (colon) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor invades subserosa or into non-peritionealized pericolic or perirectal tissues."@en 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (colon)" 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002224) 


### pT3 (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor extends into major veins or perinephric tissues but not into the ipsilateral adrenal gland and not beyond the Gerota fascia."@en 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (kidney)" 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002243) 


### pT3 (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 7 cm or one that directly invades any of: parietal pleura, chest wall (including superior sulcus tumors), diaphragm, phrenic nerve, mediastinal pleura, parietal pericardiu or the tumor is in the main bronchus less than 2 cm distal to the carina but without  involvement of the carina or there is associated atelectasis or obstructive pneumonitis of the entire lung or there is separate tumor nodule(s) in the same lobe as the primary."@en 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (lung)" 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232) 


### pT3 (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor involves one or both ovaries with microscopically confirmed peritoneal metastasis outside the pelvis and/or regional lymph node metastasis."@en 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (ovary)" 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256) 


### pT3a (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor grossly extends into the renal vein or its segmental (muscle containing) branches, or the tumor invades perirenal and/or renal sinus fat (peripelvic) fat but not beyond Gerota fascia."@en 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3a (kidney)" 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002243) 


### pT3a (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has microscopic peritoneal metastasis beyond pelvis."@en 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3a (ovary)" 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256) 


### pT3b (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253) 

- [pT3b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor grossly extends into vena cava below diaphragm."@en 

- [pT3b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3b (kidney)" 

- [pT3b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002243)

- [pT3b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002268) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has macroscopic peritoneal, metastatasis beyond pelvis, 2 cm or less in greatest dimension."@en 

- [pT3b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002268) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

- [pT3c (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002254) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor grossly extends into vena cava above the diaphragm or Invades the wall of the vena cava."@en 

- [pT3c (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002254) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

### pT3c (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002269`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002269) 

- [pT3c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002269) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has peritoneal metastasis beyond pelvis, more than 2 cm in greatest dimension and/or regional lymph node metastasis."@en

#### Added
- Individual: [pT4 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002255) 

- [pT4 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002255) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor invades beyond Gerota fascia (including contiguous extension into the ipsilateral adrenal gland)."@en
