### pT4 (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor of any size that invades any of the following: mediastinum, heart, great vessels, trachea, recurrent laryngeal nerve, esophagus, vertebral body, carina or there is separate tumor nodule(s) in a different ipsilateral lobe to that of the primary."@en 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT4 (lung)" 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232)

- Class: [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) 

- [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A partitioning data transformation is a data transformation that has objective partitioning."@en 

- [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone" 

- [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "partitioning data transformation"@en 

- [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) EquivalentTo [achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) 

- [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) SubClassOf [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) 

- [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) SubClassOf [achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) 


### partitioning objective `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172`

#### Added
- Class: [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) 

- [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A partitioning objective is a data transformation objective where the aim is to generate a collection of disjoint non-empty subsets whose union equals a non-empty input set."@en 

- [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Elisabetta Manduchi" 

- [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "partitioning objective"@en 

- [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) SubClassOf [data transformation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200166) 


### pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290`

#### Added
- Class: [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A categorical value specification that is a pathologic finding about one or more characteristics of colon cancer following the rules of the TNM AJCC v7 classification system as they pertain to distant metastases. TNM pathologic distant metastasis findings are based on clinical findings supplemented by histopathologic examination of one or more tissue specimens acquired during surgery."@en 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition" 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) SubClassOf [categorical value specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001930)

### peptide `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670`

#### Added
- Class: [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) 

- [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Amide derived from two or more amino carboxylic acid molecules (the same or different) by formation of a covalent bond from the carbonyl carbon of one to the nitrogen atom of another with formal loss of water. The term is usually applied to structures formed from &alpha;-amino acids, but it includes those derived from any amino carboxylic acid. X = OH, OR, NH2, NHR, etc." 

- [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "peptide" 

- [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) SubClassOf [macromolecule](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_33839)

### placeholder removed `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226`

#### Added
- Individual: [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) 

- [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) [editor preferred label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "placeholder removed"@en 

- [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The term was created to temporarily stand in for a semantic purpose, but is no longer needed, typically due to another permanent term being defined."@en 

- [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "This reason is appropriate for a temporary term even if no permanent term is defined; for example if the need for the term no longer exists. If a permanent (replacement) term is defined, the deprecated term should reference the replacement with IAO:0100001. If no replacement is defined, it may be appropriate to reference alternative terms with oboInOwl:consider."@en 

- [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "placeholder removed"@en 

- [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) Type [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) 

-  DifferentIndividuals: [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226), [terms merged](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000227), [term imported](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000228), [term split](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000229) 


### plan `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260`

#### Added
- Class: [plan](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260) 

- [plan](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A plan is a realizable entity that is the inheres in a bearer who is committed to realizing it as a completely executed planned process."@en 

- [plan](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "AR, BP, JM, MC, PRS"@en 

- [plan](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "plan"@en 

- [plan](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260) SubClassOf [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) 

- [plan](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260) SubClassOf [concretizes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000059) some [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104) 


### plan specification `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104`

#### Added
- Class: [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104) 

- [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A directive information entity with action specifications and objective specifications as parts, and that may be concretized as a realizable entity that, if realized, is realized in a process in which the bearer tries to achieve the objectives by taking the actions specified."@en 

- [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "plan specification"@en 

- [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104) SubClassOf [directive information entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000033) 


### planned process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000082`

#### Added
- Class: [planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000082) 

- [planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000082) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A process that is initiated by an agent who intends to carry out a plan to achieve an objective through one or more actions as described in a plan specification."@en 

- [planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000082) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "planned process"@en 

- [planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000082) SubClassOf [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015)

- [positively regulated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002336) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [Christopher J. Mungall](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165)

### positively regulates `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p positively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p increases the rate or magnitude of execution of q." 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [Christopher J. Mungall](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "positively regulates"@en 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) InverseOf [positively regulated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002336) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) SubPropertyOf: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of, positive effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002304)

### preceded by `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "x is preceded by y if and only if the time point at which y ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which x starts. Formally: x preceded by y iff &omega;(y) &lt;= &alpha;(x), where &alpha; is a function that maps a process to a start point, and &omega; is a function that maps a process to an end point."@en 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "preceded by"@en 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) InverseOf [precedes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000063) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) Domain [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) Range [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) SubPropertyOf: [ends after](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002086) 

- [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) o [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) SubPropertyOf: [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) 

-  Transitive: [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062)

### present_in_taxon `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175`
#### Removed
- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "RO:0002175" 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "applicable for taxon" 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [has_obo_namespace](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasOBONamespace) "uberon" 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "present_in_taxon" 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [is_class_level](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_class_level) true 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [is_metadata_tag](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_metadata_tag) true 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [shorthand](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#shorthand) "present_in_taxon" 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "S present_in_taxon T if some instance of T has some S. This does not means that all instances of T have an S - it may only be certain life stages or sexes that have S." 
  - [has cross-reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165" 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "The SPARQL expansion for this relation introduces new named classes into the ontology. For this reason it is likely that the expansion should only be performed during a QC pipeline; the expanded output should usually not be included in a published version of the ontology." 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "present_in_taxon" 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [see also](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#seeAlso) [Taxon-constraints](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/wiki/Taxon-constraints) 

#### Added
- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [Christopher J. Mungall](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [present_in_taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [defined by construct](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0002000) "PREFIX rdfs: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#&gt;
PREFIX owl: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#&gt;
PREFIX in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162&gt;
PREFIX present_in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175&gt;
CONSTRUCT {
  in_taxon: a owl:ObjectProperty .
  ?witness rdfs:label ?label .
  ?witness rdfs:subClassOf ?x .
  ?witness rdfs:subClassOf [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty in_taxon: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom ?taxon
  ] .
}
WHERE {
  ?x present_in_taxon: ?taxon .
  BIND(IRI(CONCAT(
    &quot;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175#&quot;,
    MD5(STR(?x)),
    &quot;-&quot;,
    MD5(STR(?taxon))
  )) as ?witness)
  BIND(CONCAT(STR(?x), &quot; in taxon &quot;, STR(?taxon)) AS ?label)
}"

- [quality of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000080) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between a quality and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the quality specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [quality of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000080) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A quality inheres in its bearer at all times for which the quality exists."@en

- [quality of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000080) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "quality of"@en 

- [quality of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000080) InverseOf [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086) 

- [quality of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000080) SubPropertyOf: [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052)

- [ready for release](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Class has undergone final review, is ready for use, and will be included in the next release. Any class lacking &quot;ready_for_release&quot; should be considered likely to change place in hierarchy, have its definition refined, or be obsoleted in the next release.  Those classes deemed &quot;ready_for_release&quot; will also derived from a chain of ancestor classes that are also &quot;ready_for_release.&quot;"@en 

- [ready for release](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "ready for release"@en

### realizable entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017`

#### Added
- Class: [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the disposition of this piece of metal to conduct electricity."@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the disposition of your blood to coagulate"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of your reproductive organs"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of being a doctor"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of this boundary to delineate where Utah and Colorado meet"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A specifically dependent continuant  that inheres in continuant  entities and are not exhibited in full at every time in which it inheres in an entity or group of entities. The exhibition or actualization of a realizable entity is a particular manifestation, functioning or process that occurs under certain circumstances."@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "realizable entity"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) DisjointWith [quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000019) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) SubClassOf [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) SubClassOf [characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000502) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) only [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) 


### realizes `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000055`

- [realizes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000055) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "realizes"@en

### regulates `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p regulates q iff p is causally upstream of q, the execution of p is not constant and varies according to specific conditions, and p influences the rate or magnitude of execution of q due to an effect either on some enabler of q or some enabler of a part of q." 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [David P Hill](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7476-6306)

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [Christopher J. Mungall](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165)

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) Range [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) o [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025) SubPropertyOf: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) 

- [directly regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002578) o [directly regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002578) SubPropertyOf: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) 

-  Transitive: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211)

### role `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023`

#### Added
- Class: [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "John&rsquo;s role of husband to Mary is dependent on Mary&rsquo;s role of wife to John, and both are dependent on the object aggregate comprising John and Mary as member parts joined together through the relational quality of being married."@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the priest role"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of a boundary to demarcate two neighboring administrative territories"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of a building in serving as a military target"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of a stone in marking a property boundary"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of subject in a clinical trial"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the student role"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A realizable entity  the manifestation of which brings about some result or end that is not essential to a continuant  in virtue of the kind of thing that it is but that can be served or participated in by that kind of continuant  in some kinds of natural, social or institutional contexts."@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: One major family of examples of non-rigid universals involves roles, and ontologies developed for corresponding administrative purposes may consist entirely of representatives of entities of this sort. Thus &lsquo;professor&rsquo;, defined as follows,b instance_of professor at t =Def. there is some c, c instance_of professor role &amp; c inheres_in b at t.denotes a non-rigid universal and so also do &lsquo;nurse&rsquo;, &lsquo;student&rsquo;, &lsquo;colonel&rsquo;, &lsquo;taxpayer&rsquo;, and so forth. (These terms are all, in the jargon of philosophy, phase sortals.) By using role terms in definitions, we can create a BFO conformant treatment of such entities drawing on the fact that, while an instance of professor may be simultaneously an instance of trade union member, no instance of the type professor role is also (at any time) an instance of the type trade union member role (any more than any instance of the type color is at any time an instance of the type length).If an ontology of employment positions should be defined in terms of roles following the above pattern, this enables the ontology to do justice to the fact that individuals instantiate the corresponding universals &ndash;  professor, sergeant, nurse &ndash; only during certain phases in their lives."@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "role"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) SubClassOf [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) 


### role of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this investigator role is a role of this person"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between a role and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the role specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A role inheres in its bearer at all times for which the role exists, however the role need not be realized at all the times that the role exists."@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "is role of"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "role_of"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "role of"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) InverseOf [has role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000087) 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) SubPropertyOf: [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) 


### scatter plot `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184`

#### Added
- Class: [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) 

- [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A scatterplot is a graph which uses Cartesian coordinates to display values for two variables for a set of data. The data is displayed as a collection of points, each having the value of one variable determining the position on the horizontal axis and the value of the other variable determining the position on the vertical axis."@en 

- [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "scatter plot"@en 

- [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) SubClassOf [graph](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000038)

- [second](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000010) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "second" 

- [second](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000010) Type [time unit](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000003)

### specifically dependent continuant `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020`

#### Added
- Class: [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Reciprocal specifically dependent continuants: the function of this key to open this lock and the mutually dependent disposition of this lock: to be opened by this key"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "of one-sided specifically dependent continuants: the mass of this tomato"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "of relational dependent continuants (multiple bearers): John&rsquo;s love for Mary, the ownership relation between John and this statue, the relation of authority between John and his subordinates."@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the disposition of this fish to decay"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of this heart: to pump blood"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the mutual dependence of proton donors and acceptors in chemical reactions [79"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the mutual dependence of the role predator and the role prey as played by two organisms in a given interaction"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the pink color of a medium rare piece of grilled filet mignon at its center"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of being a doctor"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the shape of this hole."@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the smell of this portion of mozzarella"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "b is a specifically dependent continuant = Def. b is a continuant &amp; there is some independent continuant c which is not a spatial region and which is such that b s-depends_on c at every time t during the course of b&rsquo;s existence. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [050-003])"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "specifically dependent continuant"@en 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) DisjointWith [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) SubClassOf [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) only [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) 


### specimen `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051`

#### Added
- Class: [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) 

- [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A material entity that is collected for potential use as an input upon which measurements or observations are performed."@en 

- [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: James Malone" 

- [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Philippe Rocca-Serra" 

- [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "specimen"@en 

- [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) EquivalentTo [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) and ([has role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000087) some [specimen role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000112)) 

- [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) SubClassOf [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) SubClassOf [has role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000087) some [specimen role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000112) 


### specimen collection objective `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000684`

### specimen collection process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659`

#### Added
- Class: [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A planned process with the objective to obtain a material entity for potential use as an input upon which measurements or observations are performed."@en 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Bjoern Peters" 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "specimen collection process" 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) EquivalentTo [completely executed planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000035) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [specimen collection objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000684)) 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) SubClassOf [completely executed planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000035) 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) SubClassOf [has specified input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000293) some [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) SubClassOf [has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) 

- [specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) SubClassOf [achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [specimen collection objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000684) 


### specimen from organism `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479`

#### Added
- Class: [specimen from organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479) 

- [specimen from organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A specimen that derives from an anatomical part or substance arising from an organism. Examples of tissue specimen include tissue, organ, physiological system, blood, or body location (arm)."@en 

- [specimen from organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Chris Stoeckert, Jie Zheng" 

- [specimen from organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "specimen from organism" 

- [specimen from organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479) EquivalentTo [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) and ([is specified output of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000312) some 
([specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) and ([has specified input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000293) some 
([organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000022) or [material anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000465) or [anatomical cluster](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000477))))) 

- [specimen from organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479) SubClassOf [specimen](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051) 

- [specimen from organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001479) SubClassOf [is specified output of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000312) some 
([specimen collection process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000659) and ([has specified input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000293) some 
([organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000022) or [material anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000465) or [anatomical cluster](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000477))))

### spinal cerebrospinal fluid flow rate `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00352`
#### Removed

### textual definition `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115`
#### Removed
- [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2012-04-05: 
Barry Smith

The official OBI definition, explaining the meaning of a class or property: 'Shall be Aristotelian, formalized and normalized. Can be augmented with colloquial definitions'  is terrible.

Can you fix to something like:

A statement of necessary and sufficient conditions explaining the meaning of an expression referring to a class or property.

Alan Ruttenberg

Your proposed definition is a reasonable candidate, except that it is very common that necessary and sufficient conditions are not given. Mostly they are necessary, occasionally they are necessary and sufficient or just sufficient. Often they use terms that are not themselves defined and so they effectively can't be evaluated by those criteria. 

On the specifics of the proposed definition:

We don't have definitions of 'meaning' or 'expression' or 'property'. For 'reference' in the intended sense I think we use the term 'denotation'. For 'expression', I think we you mean symbol, or identifier. For 'meaning' it differs for class and property. For class we want documentation that let's the intended reader determine whether an entity is instance of the class, or not. For property we want documentation that let's the intended reader determine, given a pair of potential relata, whether the assertion that the relation holds is true. The 'intended reader' part suggests that we also specify who, we expect, would be able to understand the definition, and also generalizes over human and computer reader to include textual and logical definition. 

Personally, I am more comfortable weakening definition to documentation, with instructions as to what is desirable. 

We also have the outstanding issue of how to aim different definitions to different audiences. A clinical audience reading chebi wants a different sort of definition documentation/definition from a chemistry trained audience, and similarly there is a need for a definition that is adequate for an ontologist to work with.  "@en 

- [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [iao.owl](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/iao.owl) 

#### Added
- [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A property representing the English language definitions of what NCI means by the concept. They may also include information about the definition's source and attribution in a form that can easily be interpreted by software." 

- [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "English language definitions of what NCI means by the concept. These are limited to 1024 characters. They may also include information about the definition's source and attribution in a form that can easily be interpreted by software." 

- [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The official OBI definition, explaining the meaning of a class or property. Shall be Aristotelian, formalized and normalized. Can be augmented with colloquial definitions."@en 

- [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The official definition." 

- [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "textual definition"

### universal `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410`

#### Added
- Individual: [universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) 

- [universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) [editor preferred label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "universal"@en 

- [universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "Hard to give a definition for. Intuitively a &quot;natural kind&quot; rather than a collection of any old things, which a class is able to be, formally. At the meta level, universals are defined as positives, are disjoint with their siblings, have single asserted parents."@en 

- [universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "A Formal Theory of Substances, Qualities, and Universals, http://ontology.buffalo.edu/bfo/SQU.pdf"@en 

- [universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "universal"@en 

- [universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) Type [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409)
